text.title: LB Layer Selection Procedure
text.summary: 5-step procedure to apply the lb-layer-selection methodology end-to-end.

step.name: capture-traffic-profile
step.input: Protocol mix, content-routing rules, TLS requirements, anycast need.
step.action: List traffic types (HTTP / gRPC / TCP / UDP) + content-routing rules + TLS strategy. Record performance target (rps, conns/s).

step.name: check-feature-vs-layer-consistency
step.action: For each feature, mark required layer: path-routing → L7, raw-TCP → L4, anycast → L4 (BGP), waf → L7, header-rewrite → L7. Inconsistency triggers `no-l4-with-l7-features` rule.
step.decision-gate: If any feature contradicts the proposed layer, STOP and redesign.

step.name: pick-layer
step.action: If all features fit one layer, use it. If features span L4 + L7, use hybrid L4-front-of-L7. Set `tls_strategy` consistent with the layer.

step.name: emit-decision-record
step.action: Render `templates/layer-decision-record.md` capturing the choice + per-feature mapping + rationale. Commit as ADR.

step.name: validate
step.action: Run `validate-lb-layer-selection.py --file decision.json`. Reject contradictions; pass clean decisions on.
